description_long: This guard stone is a robust, mushroom-shaped object crafted from a light gray stone material. Its design features a broad, flared base with a rough, uneven edge, providing stability and a visual deterrent to vehicles. The midsection tapers inward before expanding into a domed, cap-like top, reminiscent of traditional European guard stones used to protect building corners and pedestrian areas. The surface is matte and textured, with visible striations and subtle color variations, suggesting exposure to the elements. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, emphasizing its utilitarian function. The overall form is solid and heavy, intended to serve as a physical barrier in urban environments.
description: A solid, mushroom-shaped stone guard stone with a wide, flared base and domed top, featuring a rough, matte gray surface.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broad, flared base and the domed top, with the tapering midsection clearly visible and the surface texture prominent.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the uneven edge of the base and the pronounced overhang of the cap, with slight asymmetry on the right side.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the full curvature of the cap and the base, with the surface striations and roughness most apparent.
description_view_3: This angle emphasizes the gradual taper of the midsection and the consistent texture across the stone's surface.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, near building entrances, or at the edges of sidewalks to prevent vehicle encroachment and protect pedestrians.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, with the base resting flush on a paved or stone surface.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to exhibit chipping and smoothing along the base edge from vehicle contact, as well as surface discoloration and erosion due to weather exposure. The top and sides may show minor pitting or lichen growth from prolonged outdoor placement.
